{"parentUuid":null,"isSidechain":true,"promptId":"1cc8c58e-5118-404c-b220-d25731c48877","agentId":"ad6731f24328d00a5","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":"You are a historical research assistant. Do heavy web research on ALL major territorial/border changes from 1860 to 1920. This is for an academic project tracking \"polities\" (political entities defined by territory over time).\n\nFor each event, provide: exact year, territories involved, nature of change (unification, secession, annexation, cession, conquest), area affected (km²) if possible, and the resulting new polities.\n\nCover EXHAUSTIVELY:\n1. **Italian Unification (1860-1870)**: Kingdom of Two Sicilies annexed 1860, Duchy of Modena 1860, Duchy of Parma 1860, Tuscany 1860, Sardinia becomes Italy 1861, Venetia from Austria 1866, Papal States/Rome 1870\n2. **German Unification (1864-1871)**: Schleswig-Holstein 1864, North German Confederation 1867, Franco-Prussian War 1870-71, German Empire proclaimed 1871, Alsace-Lorraine annexation\n3. **Balkans**: Serbia independence 1878, Romania independence 1878, Bulgaria autonomy 1878/independence 1908, Montenegro independence 1878, Balkan Wars 1912-1913 (territory changes for Serbia, Greece, Bulgaria, Romania, Albania creation, Ottoman losses), Bosnia-Herzegovina annexation by Austria-Hungary 1908\n4. **Africa colonization**: Scramble for Africa post-Berlin Conference 1884-85, all major colonial boundary changes, British, French, German, Portuguese, Belgian, Italian, Spanish territories\n5. **Asia**: Durand Line 1893 (Afghanistan/British India), Siam/Thailand territorial losses to France/Britain 1893-1909, Japanese expansion (Taiwan 1895, Korea 1910), China territorial losses\n6. **Americas**: Alaska Purchase 1867, Panama independence from Colombia 1903, boundary disputes (Brazil-Bolivia Acre 1903, Peru-Ecuador, Chile-Bolivia-Peru War of Pacific 1879-1884)\n7. **Ottoman Empire dissolution begins**: Egypt British protectorate, Cyprus to Britain 1878, Bosnia to Austria-Hungary 1908\n8. **WWI aftermath (1918-1920)**: Treaty of Versailles, Treaty of Trianon, Treaty of Sèvres/Lausanne, creation of Poland, Czechoslovakia, Yugoslavia, Finland, Baltic states, Alsace-Lorraine return to France, Austria-Hungary dissolution, Ottoman Empire collapse, mandates (Palestine, Syria, Lebanon, Iraq, Transjordan)\n9. **Russian Empire/Soviet changes**: Finland independence 1917, Baltic states 1918, Poland reconstitution 1918, Bessarabia to Romania 1918\n\nFor each change give the EXACT year and territories. Be exhaustive. This will be used to verify an academic database."},"uuid":"130c9df4-8ba1-43c8-a8e9-89eaeb7dc14a","timestamp":"2026-03-24T20:15:01.772Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/catalin/WHEP-refactor-polities","sessionId":"b5ea2b37-3948-44f5-9e01-2ef45e84c64c","version":"2.1.81","gitBranch":"lbm364dl/refactor-polities","slug":"sequential-inventing-treehouse"}
{"parentUuid":"130c9df4-8ba1-43c8-a8e9-89eaeb7dc14a","isSidechain":true,"agentId":"ad6731f24328d00a5","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_01Vyn5rGeinRRPbdYG8ka8Lt","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"text","text":"\n\nI'll compile a comprehensive chronological catalog of major territorial/border changes from 1860 to 1920. Since I cannot perform web searches, I'll draw on my extensive training knowledge of this period. Let me organize this systematically.\n\n---\n\n# EXHAUSTIVE CATALOG OF MAJOR TERRITORIAL/BORDER CHANGES, 1860–1920\n\n---\n\n## 1. ITALIAN UNIFICATION (1859–1870)\n\n| Year | Event | Nature | Territories | Area (approx. km²) | Resulting Polity |\n|------|-------|--------|-------------|---------------------|------------------|\n| 1859 | Treaty of Zurich / Treaty of Villafranca | Cession (Austria to Sardinia, via France) | Lombardy | ~22,000 | Kingdom of Sardinia absorbs Lombardy |\n| 1860 (Mar) | Annexation via plebiscite | Annexation/Unification | Duchy of Parma | ~6,200 | Merged into Sardinia |\n| 1860 (Mar) | Annexation via plebiscite | Annexation/Unification | Duchy of Modena and Reggio | ~6,400 | Merged into Sardinia |\n| 1860 (Mar) | Annexation via plebiscite | Annexation/Unification | Grand Duchy of Tuscany | ~22,000 | Merged into Sardinia |\n| 1860 (Mar) | Annexation via plebiscite | Annexation/Unification | Papal Legations (Romagna, part of Papal States) | ~15,000 | Merged into Sardinia |\n| 1860 (Mar) | Treaty of Turin | Cession (Sardinia to France) | Savoy and Nice (County of Nice) | Savoy ~10,400; Nice ~4,000 | Transferred to France |\n| 1860 (Sep–Nov) | Garibaldi's Expedition / Plebiscite | Conquest then Annexation | Kingdom of the Two Sicilies (Naples + Sicily) | ~111,900 | Merged into Sardinia |\n| 1860 (Nov) | Invasion + Plebiscite | Annexation | Marche and Umbria (Papal States minus Rome/Lazio) | ~20,000 | Merged into Sardinia |\n| **1861 (Mar 17)** | **Proclamation of the Kingdom of Italy** | **Unification** | All above territories | ~250,000 | **Kingdom of Italy** (Sardinia renamed/reconstituted) |\n| 1866 | Treaty of Vienna (after Third Italian War of Independence / Austro-Prussian War) | Cession (Austria to Italy, via France as intermediary) | Venetia (Veneto, Friuli, Mantua) | ~36,000 | Incorporated into Kingdom of Italy |\n| 1870 (Sep 20) | Capture of Rome (Breach of Porta Pia) | Conquest/Annexation | Papal States remnant (Rome and Lazio) | ~12,000 | Incorporated into Italy; Rome becomes capital (1871). Pope retains Vatican informally. |\n\n**Net result by 1871:** Kingdom of Italy (~310,000 km²). Papal sovereignty reduced to the Vatican question (unresolved until Lateran Treaty, 1929).\n\n---\n\n## 2. GERMAN UNIFICATION (1864–1871)\n\n| Year | Event | Nature | Territories | Area (approx. km²) | Resulting Polity |\n|------|-------|--------|-------------|---------------------|------------------|\n| 1864 | Second Schleswig War; Treaty of Vienna (Oct 30) | Conquest/Cession (Denmark to Prussia & Austria) | Duchies of Schleswig, Holstein, and Lauenburg | Schleswig ~9,000; Holstein ~9,500; Lauenburg ~1,200 | Jointly administered by Prussia (Schleswig) and Austria (Holstein) |\n| 1865 | Convention of Gastein (Aug) | Administrative partition | Schleswig, Holstein, Lauenburg | (same) | Prussia administers Schleswig; Austria administers Holstein; Prussia buys Lauenburg from Austria |\n| 1866 | Austro-Prussian War; Treaty of Prague (Aug 23) | Conquest/Annexation | Holstein (from Austria); Kingdom of Hanover; Electorate of Hesse-Kassel (Hesse); Duchy of Nassau; Free City of Frankfurt | Hanover ~38,500; Hesse-Kassel ~9,600; Nassau ~4,700; Frankfurt ~100 | All annexed directly into Prussia |\n| 1867 (Jul 1) | North German Confederation formed | Federation (Prussian-led) | 22 German states north of the Main River | ~415,000 | North German Confederation (Prussia dominant) |\n| 1870–71 | Franco-Prussian War | Conquest | — | — | — |\n| 1871 (Jan 18) | Proclamation of German Empire at Versailles | Unification/Federation | North German Confederation + Bavaria, Württemberg, Baden, Hesse-Darmstadt (south of Main) | ~540,000 total | **German Empire** (Deutsches Reich) |\n| 1871 (May 10) | Treaty of Frankfurt | Annexation/Cession (France to Germany) | Alsace and parts of Lorraine (Moselle) | ~14,500 (Reichsland Elsaß-Lothringen) | Alsace-Lorraine becomes Imperial Territory (Reichsland) of Germany |\n\n**Additional note:** The Kingdom of Prussia within the Empire comprised roughly 65% of German territory (~350,000 km²) after its 1866 annexations.\n\n---\n\n## 3. BALKANS (1875–1913)\n\n### Congress of Berlin, 1878 (Treaty of Berlin, July 13)\n\n| Year | Event | Nature | Territories | Area (approx. km²) | Resulting Polity |\n|------|-------|--------|-------------|---------------------|------------------|\n| 1878 | Treaty of Berlin | Independence (from Ottoman Empire) | Principality of Serbia (expanded) | ~48,300 (gains of ~11,000 from Ottomans: Niš, Pirot, Vranje, Toplica) | Principality of Serbia (Kingdom from 1882) |\n| 1878 | Treaty of Berlin | Independence (from Ottoman Empire) | Principality of Romania (expanded) | ~130,000 total; loses Southern Bessarabia (~6,000) to Russia, gains Northern Dobruja (~15,500) from Ottomans | Principality of Romania (Kingdom from 1881) |\n| 1878 | Treaty of Berlin | Autonomy (within Ottoman Empire) | Principality of Bulgaria | ~64,000 | Autonomous Principality of Bulgaria (tributary to Ottomans) |\n| 1878 | Treaty of Berlin | Autonomy (Ottoman province, special status) | Eastern Rumelia | ~33,000 | Semi-autonomous Ottoman province |\n| 1878 | Treaty of Berlin | Independence (from Ottoman Empire) | Principality of Montenegro (expanded) | ~9,500 (gains of ~4,900: Nikšić, Bar, Podgorica, parts of Herzegovina) | Principality of Montenegro (Kingdom from 1910) |\n| 1878 | Treaty of Berlin | Occupation/Administration (Austria-Hungary occupies, Ottoman sovereignty nominal) | Bosnia and Herzegovina | ~51,000 | Administered by Austria-Hungary |\n| 1878 | Treaty of Berlin (Convention of Cyprus) | Administration (Ottoman to Britain) | Cyprus | ~9,250 | British-administered (formally Ottoman until 1914 annexation) |\n| 1878 | Treaty of Berlin | Cession (Ottoman to Russia) | Kars, Ardahan, Batumi (southwestern Caucasus) | ~25,000 | Incorporated into Russian Empire (Kars Oblast) |\n| 1878 | Treaty of Berlin | Cession (Romania to Russia) | Southern Bessarabia (Budjak) | ~6,000 | Returned to Russian Empire |\n\n### Post-1878 Balkan Changes\n\n| Year | Event | Nature | Territories | Area (approx. km²) | Resulting Polity |\n|------|-------|--------|-------------|---------------------|------------------|\n| 1881 | Convention of Constantinople | Cession (Ottoman to Greece) | Thessaly and part of Epirus (Arta region) | ~13,400 | Incorporated into Kingdom of Greece |\n| 1885 | Unification of Bulgaria and Eastern Rumelia | Unification | Eastern Rumelia merged with Bulgaria | +33,000 | Principality of Bulgaria (~97,000 total) |\n| 1897 | Greco-Turkish War | No territorial change (status quo ante) | Thessaly briefly occupied by Ottomans | — | Greece retains Thessaly (pays indemnity) |\n| 1908 (Oct 5) | Bulgarian Declaration of Independence | Independence (from Ottoman suzerainty) | Bulgaria | ~97,000 | Kingdom of Bulgaria (Tsardom) |\n| 1908 (Oct 6) | Bosnian Crisis | Annexation (Austria-Hungary formally annexes, previously occupied) | Bosnia and Herzegovina | ~51,000 | Formally part of Austria-Hungary |\n| 1908 | Cretan unification | De facto union | Crete | ~8,300 | De facto united with Greece (formal recognition 1913) |\n\n### Balkan Wars (1912–1913)\n\n**First Balkan War (Oct 1912 – May 1913) — Treaty of London (May 30, 1913):**\nOttoman Empire loses nearly all European territories.\n\n**Second Balkan War (Jun–Aug 1913) — Treaty of Bucharest (Aug 10, 1913):**\n\n| Year | Event / Treaty | Nature | Territories Gained | Area (approx. km²) | Resulting Polity |\n|------|---------------|--------|-------------------|---------------------|------------------|\n| 1913 | Treaty of Bucharest | Conquest/Cession from Ottomans & Bulgaria | **Serbia gains:** Kosovo, northern/central Macedonia (Vardar Macedonia), Sandžak of Novi Pazar (shared with Montenegro) | ~39,000 gained | Kingdom of Serbia (~87,000 total) |\n| 1913 | Treaty of Bucharest | Conquest/Cession from Ottomans & Bulgaria | **Greece gains:** Southern Macedonia (including Thessaloniki/Salonica), southern Epirus, Crete (formally), Aegean islands | ~68,000 gained | Kingdom of Greece (~120,000 total) |\n| 1913 | Treaty of Bucharest | Net loss (won some from Ottomans, lost to neighbors) | **Bulgaria gains:** Western Thrace (Aegean coastline), Pirin Macedonia; **loses:** Southern Dobruja to Romania, most of Macedonia to Serbia/Greece, Adrianople (Edirne) back to Ottomans | Net gain ~15,000 from Ottomans; lost ~29,000 to Serbia, Greece, Romania | Kingdom of Bulgaria (~111,000 total) |\n| 1913 | Treaty of Bucharest | Cession from Bulgaria | **Romania gains:** Southern Dobruja (Cadrilater) | ~7,700 | Kingdom of Romania (~138,000 total) |\n| 1913 | Treaty of Bucharest | Conquest from Ottomans | **Montenegro gains:** Part of Sandžak of Novi Pazar, part of northern Albania (Metohija area) | ~5,000 gained | Kingdom of Montenegro (~14,500 total) |\n| 1913 (Nov) | Treaty of London / Conference of Ambassadors | Creation (from former Ottoman territory) | **Albania created** | ~28,000 | Principality of Albania (independent state) |\n| 1913 | Treaty of Constantinople (Sep 29) | Cession (Bulgaria to Ottoman Empire) | **Ottoman Empire recovers:** Adrianople (Edirne) and eastern Thrace | ~24,000 | Ottoman Empire retains foothold in Europe (~24,000 km² in Thrace) |\n\n---\n\n## 4. AFRICA: COLONIZATION AND THE SCRAMBLE FOR AFRICA (1860–1920)\n\n### Pre-Berlin Conference\n\n| Year | Event | Nature | Territory | Area (approx. km²) | Colonial Power |\n|------|-------|--------|-----------|---------------------|---------------|\n| 1862 | Treaty of Fez | Protectorate (partial) | Parts of Morocco (Spanish enclaves expanded) | Small coastal areas | Spain |\n| 1862 | French acquisition | Colony | Djibouti (Obock) | ~23,000 (eventually) | France (French Somaliland) |\n| 1868 | British annexation | Annexation | Basutoland (Lesotho) | ~30,000 | Britain |\n| 1871 | British annexation | Annexation | Griqualand West (diamond fields) | ~40,000 | Britain (Cape Colony) |\n| 1874 | Gold Coast | Colony formalized | Gold Coast interior (Ashanti wars) | ~239,000 (eventually) | Britain |\n| 1878 | Leopold II / International Association of the Congo | Private colonial venture | Congo Basin (claimed) | ~2,345,000 (eventually) | Leopold II (personal) |\n| 1881 | Treaty of Bardo | Protectorate | Tunisia | ~164,000 | France |\n| 1882 | British occupation | Military occupation (formal Ottoman/Khedival sovereignty) | Egypt | ~1,000,000 | Britain (de facto) |\n| 1884 | German protectorate declared | Colony | Togoland | ~90,000 | Germany |\n| 1884 | German protectorate declared | Colony | Kamerun (Cameroon) | ~790,000 (eventually) | Germany |\n| 1884 | German protectorate declared | Colony | German South West Africa (Namibia) | ~835,000 | Germany |\n| 1884 | German protectorate declared | Colony | German East Africa (Tanganyika) | ~995,000 | Germany |\n| 1884 | Spanish claim formalized | Colony | Spanish Sahara (Río de Oro) | ~266,000 | Spain |\n| 1884 | Spanish protectorate | Colony | Spanish Guinea (Equatorial Guinea) | ~28,000 | Spain |\n\n### Berlin Conference (Nov 1884 – Feb 1885) and aftermath\n\n| Year | Event | Nature | Territory | Area (approx. km²) | Colonial Power |\n|------|-------|--------|-----------|---------------------|---------------|\n| 1885 | General Act of Berlin Conference | International recognition | Congo Free State | ~2,345,000 | Leopold II (personal sovereignty) |\n| 1885 | Berlin Conference framework | Protectorate | Bechuanaland (Botswana) | ~582,000 | Britain |\n| 1885 | Berlin Conference framework | Protectorate/Sphere | Niger territories (Nigeria) | ~924,000 (eventually) | Britain (Royal Niger Company) |\n| 1885 | Berlin Conference framework | Colony | French Congo (Moyen-Congo, Gabon) | ~2,000,000+ (eventually part of French Equatorial Africa) | France |\n| 1886 | Anglo-German Agreement (Oct 29) | Partition/Boundary | East Africa spheres: Tanganyika (Germany) vs. Kenya/Uganda (Britain) | — | Britain/Germany |\n| 1886 | Anglo-German Agreement | Colony confirmed | Wituland / Kenya coast | ~580,000 (British East Africa eventually) | Britain |\n| 1887 | British protectorate | Colony | British Somaliland | ~176,000 | Britain |\n| 1888 | British protectorate | Colony | Matabeleland & Mashonaland (Southern Rhodesia/Zimbabwe) — via BSAC charter | ~390,000 | Britain (BSAC) |\n| 1889 | Italian protectorate formalized | Colony | Italian Somaliland | ~500,000 | Italy |\n| 1889 | Treaty of Wuchale/Uccialli | Protectorate (claimed by Italy, disputed) | Ethiopia (claimed) | ~1,100,000 | Italy (claim rejected by Ethiopia) |\n| 1889–90 | British South Africa Company | Colony | Northern Rhodesia (Zambia) | ~753,000 | Britain (BSAC) |\n| 1890 | Heligoland-Zanzibar Treaty (Jul 1) | Exchange/Cession | Germany cedes Zanzibar claims, Wituland, to Britain; Britain cedes Heligoland to Germany; Caprivi Strip to Germany | Zanzibar ~2,600; Heligoland ~1.7; Caprivi ~20,000 | Britain gets Zanzibar protectorate; Germany gets Heligoland + Caprivi |\n| 1890 | Anglo-French Convention | Boundary | Niger-Lake Chad boundary; Madagascar recognized as French sphere | — | France/Britain |\n| 1890 | British protectorate | Colony | Uganda | ~241,000 | Britain |\n| 1891 | Anglo-Portuguese Treaty (Jun 11) | Boundary | Mozambique/Rhodesia borders; Nyasaland (Malawi) confirmed British | Nyasaland ~118,000 | Britain/Portugal |\n| 1891 | Franco-Portuguese boundary | Boundary | Guinea-Bissau / French Guinea border | — | France/Portugal |\n| 1893 | Franco-Siamese crisis | — (See Asia section) | — | — | — |\n| 1894 | French colony | Conquest/Colony | Dahomey (Benin) | ~113,000 | France |\n| 1894 | French protectorate | Colony | Madagascar | ~587,000 | France (full colony 1896) |\n| 1895 | British colony | Colony/Protectorate | British East Africa Protectorate (Kenya) | ~580,000 | Britain |\n| 1895 | French colony | Conquest | French West Africa (AOF) federation formed | ~4,700,000 total | France |\n| 1896 | Battle of Adwa (Mar 1) | Failed conquest / Ethiopian victory | Ethiopia remains independent | ~1,100,000 | Ethiopia (Treaty of Addis Ababa; Italy recognizes independence) |\n| 1898 | Fashoda Incident (Sep-Nov) | Diplomatic crisis resolved | Upper Nile / Sudan | — | Britain prevails; France withdraws claims |\n| 1899 | Anglo-Egyptian Condominium | Condominium | Sudan | ~2,506,000 | Britain/Egypt (effectively British) |\n| 1899–1902 | Second Boer War | Conquest/Annexation | South African Republic (Transvaal) ~287,000 + Orange Free State ~130,000 | ~417,000 | Britain (annexed as Crown Colonies 1902) |\n| 1900 | British colony | Colony/Protectorate | Northern Nigeria; Southern Nigeria | ~924,000 total | Britain |\n\n### Post-1900 Africa\n\n| Year | Event | Nature | Territory | Area (approx. km²) | Colonial Power |\n|------|-------|--------|-----------|---------------------|---------------|\n| 1904 | Entente Cordiale (Apr 8) | Diplomatic agreement | Britain recognizes French position in Morocco; France recognizes British position in Egypt | — | Britain/France |\n| 1905 | Congo Free State reforms begin | — | — | — | International pressure on Leopold |\n| 1906 | Act of Algeciras | International agreement | Morocco | ~447,000 | French/Spanish spheres confirmed |\n| 1908 | Belgian annexation | Transfer (personal to state) | Congo Free State becomes Belgian Congo | ~2,345,000 | Belgium |\n| 1910 | Union of South Africa formed | Unification (British colonies) | Cape Colony, Natal, Transvaal, Orange River Colony | ~1,221,000 | Union of South Africa (British Dominion) |\n| 1911 | Agadir Crisis; Franco-German Treaty (Nov 4) | Exchange/Cession | France gets free hand in Morocco; Germany gets Neukamerun (parts of French Congo added to Kamerun) | Neukamerun ~275,000 | Germany gains territory; France gains Morocco |\n| 1911 | Italian invasion | Conquest | Libya (Tripolitania + Cyrenaica + Fezzan) | ~1,760,000 | Italy (Treaty of Ouchy 1912; Ottoman cession) |\n| 1912 | Treaty of Fez (Mar 30) | Protectorate | French Morocco | ~415,000 | France |\n| 1912 | Franco-Spanish Convention (Nov 27) | Protectorate | Spanish Morocco (northern strip + Ifni) | ~20,000 | Spain |\n| 1912 | Spanish protectorate | Colony | Tangier International Zone (separate status) | ~380 | International |\n| 1914 | British protectorate formalized | Protectorate | Egypt (severed from Ottoman Empire upon WWI) | ~1,000,000 | Britain |\n| 1914 | Amalgamation | Colony | Northern + Southern Nigeria merged into Colony of Nigeria | ~924,000 | Britain |\n| 1916 | Sykes-Picot (affects Africa peripherally) | — | — | — | — |\n| 1919–20 | Treaty of Versailles / League of Nations Mandates | Mandates (former German colonies redistributed) | — | — | — |\n| 1920 | League Mandate | Mandate (Class B) | German East Africa → Tanganyika (Britain); Ruanda-Urundi (Belgium) | Tanganyika ~995,000; Ruanda-Urundi ~54,000 | Britain; Belgium |\n| 1920 | League Mandate | Mandate (Class C/B) | German South West Africa → South West Africa | ~835,000 | South Africa (mandate) |\n| 1920 | League Mandate | Mandate (Class B) | Kamerun → Cameroons: French Cameroun (~430,000) + British Cameroons (~90,000) | ~520,000 (split) | France; Britain |\n| 1920 | League Mandate | Mandate (Class B) | Togoland → French Togoland (~57,000) + British Togoland (~33,000) | ~90,000 (split) | France; Britain |\n\n---\n\n## 5. ASIA (1860–1920)\n\n### Japan\n\n| Year | Event | Nature | Territory | Area (approx. km²) | Resulting Polity |\n|------|-------|--------|-----------|---------------------|------------------|\n| 1868 | Meiji Restoration | Internal transformation | Japan | ~378,000 | Empire of Japan |\n| 1869 | Hokkaido colonization | Internal colonization | Hokkaido (Ezo) | ~83,000 | Formally integrated into Japan |\n| 1872 | Annexation | Annexation | Ryukyu Islands (Okinawa) | ~2,300 | Incorporated into Japan (Ryukyu Kingdom dissolved 1879) |\n| 1876 | Japan-Korea Treaty of Ganghwa | Opening/Sphere of influence | Korea (Joseon) | — | Korea opened to Japanese trade |\n| 1895 | Treaty of Shimonoseki (Apr 17, post-First Sino-Japanese War) | Cession (China to Japan) | Taiwan (Formosa) + Pescadores (Penghu) | Taiwan ~36,000; Pescadores ~127 | Japanese colony |\n| 1895 | Treaty of Shimonoseki | Cession then retrocession | Liaodong Peninsula (initially ceded, then retroceded under Triple Intervention) | ~3,500 | Returned to China (Nov 1895) |\n| 1905 | Treaty of Portsmouth (Sep 5, post-Russo-Japanese War) | Cession (Russia to Japan) | Southern Sakhalin (south of 50°N) | ~36,000 | Japanese territory (Karafuto) |\n| 1905 | Treaty of Portsmouth | Lease transfer (Russia to Japan) | Kwantung Leased Territory (Liaodong Peninsula including Port Arthur/Dalian) | ~3,500 | Japanese leasehold |\n| 1905 | Japan-Korea Treaty (Nov 17) | Protectorate | Korea | ~221,000 | Japanese protectorate |\n| 1910 (Aug 22) | Japan-Korea Annexation Treaty | Annexation | Korea (Joseon/Korean Empire) | ~221,000 | Japanese colony (Chōsen) |\n| 1914 | Siege of Tsingtao (WWI) | Conquest | Kiautschou Bay concession (German leasehold in Shandong, China) | ~552 | Japanese occupation (confirmed at Versailles 1919) |\n| 1914 | WWI conquest | Conquest | German Pacific Islands: Mariana, Caroline, Marshall Islands | ~2,500 (land area) | Japanese mandate (South Seas Mandate, League of Nations 1920) |\n\n### China — Territorial Losses\n\n| Year | Event | Nature | Territory | Area (approx. km²) | Resulting Polity |\n|------|-------|--------|-----------|---------------------|------------------|\n| 1860 | Convention of Peking (Oct 18) | Cession (China to Russia) | Outer Manchuria / Primorsky (including site of Vladivostok) | ~400,000 | Russian Empire |\n| 1860 | Convention of Peking | Cession | Kowloon Peninsula (addition to Hong Kong) | ~12 | Britain |\n| 1862 | Treaty of Saigon | Cession (Vietnam/China tributary to France) | Cochinchina (southern Vietnam, 3 provinces initially) | ~60,000 (eventually) | France |\n| 1884–85 | Sino-French War / Treaty of Tientsin | Cession of suzerainty | Vietnam (Tonkin + Annam) — China renounces suzerainty | ~330,000 | French Indochina (protectorate) |\n| 1886 | Third Anglo-Burmese War | Annexation | Burma — China loses tributary | ~677,000 | British Burma (part of British India) |\n| 1895 | Treaty of Shimonoseki | Cession | Taiwan, Pescadores (see Japan above); China recognizes Korean independence | — | — |\n| 1897–98 | Scramble for Concessions | Leases (forced) | Kiautschou Bay (Germany); Kwantung/Port Arthur (Russia); Weihaiwei (Britain); Guangzhouwan (France) | Various small areas | Various European powers + Japan |\n| 1898 | Convention for the Extension of Hong Kong Territory | Lease (99 years) | New Territories (Hong Kong expansion) | ~976 | Britain |\n| 1900–01 | Boxer Rebellion aftermath | No territorial cession but | Legation Quarter in Peking expanded; indemnity | — | Multi-power |\n| 1911 | Mongolian independence declared | Secession | Outer Mongolia | ~1,564,000 | Bogd Khanate of Mongolia (autonomous; Chinese suzerainty contested) |\n| 1913 | Simla Convention (1914) | Autonomy recognized | Tibet (de facto independent) | ~1,228,000 | De facto independent Tibet |\n\n### Siam (Thailand)\n\n| Year | Event | Nature | Territory | Area (approx. km²) | Resulting Polity |\n|------|-------|--------|-----------|---------------------|------------------|\n| 1863 | French protectorate | Protectorate | Cambodia (Siamese tributary lost) | ~181,000 | France (French Indochina) |\n| 1893 | Franco-Siamese War / Treaty (Oct 3) | Cession (Siam to France) | All territory east of the Mekong (Laos) | ~236,800 | France (French Laos, part of Indochina) |\n| 1904 | Franco-Siamese Treaty (Feb 13) | Cession (Siam to France) | Territory on west bank of Mekong (Luang Prabang, Champasak areas) | ~25,000 | France |\n| 1907 | Franco-Siamese Treaty (Mar 23) | Cession (Siam to France) | Battambang, Siem Reap, Sisophon (western Cambodia provinces) | ~50,000 | France (Indochina) |\n| 1909 | Anglo-Siamese Treaty (Mar 10) | Cession (Siam to Britain) | Four northern Malay states: Kelantan, Terengganu, Kedah, Perlis | ~36,000 | Britain (Unfederated Malay States) |\n\n### Afghanistan / Central Asia\n\n| Year | Event | Nature | Territory | Area (approx. km²) | Resulting Polity |\n|------|-------|--------|-----------|---------------------|------------------|\n| 1893 | Durand Line Agreement (Nov 12) | Boundary demarcation | Afghan-British India border (splitting Pashtun territories) | — (boundary, not cession per se) | Afghanistan / British India border fixed |\n| 1895 | Pamir Boundary Commission | Boundary | Wakhan Corridor defined as Afghan buffer between Russia and British India | ~10,000 (Wakhan) | Afghanistan |\n| 1907 | Anglo-Russian Convention (Aug 31) | Spheres of influence | Afghanistan, Persia, Tibet — spheres defined | — | Afghanistan in British sphere; Persia divided |\n\n### Southeast Asia — Other\n\n| Year | Event | Nature | Territory | Area (approx. km²) | Resulting Polity |\n|------|-------|--------|-----------|---------------------|------------------|\n| 1862 | Treaty of Saigon | Cession | Cochinchina (3 eastern provinces) | ~40,000 | France |\n| 1867 | French expansion | Annexation | Cochinchina (3 western provinces added) | ~20,000 additional | France (full Cochinchina colony) |\n| 1863 | French protectorate | Protectorate | Cambodia | ~181,000 | France |\n| 1883–84 | Treaties of Huế | Protectorate | Annam and Tonkin (Vietnam) | ~330,000 | France (Indochina) |\n| 1887 | French Indochina formed | Federation | Cochinchina + Annam + Tonkin + Cambodia | ~737,000 | French Indochina |\n| 1893 | Added to Indochina | Protectorate | Laos | ~237,000 | French Indochina |\n| 1898 | Spanish-American War / Treaty of Paris (Dec 10) | Cession (Spain to USA) | Philippines | ~300,000 | United States (colony) |\n| 1898 | Spanish-American War / Treaty of Paris | Cession (Spain to USA) | Guam | ~540 | United States |\n| 1899 | German-Spanish Treaty | Purchase | Caroline Islands, Mariana Islands (minus Guam), Palau | ~2,500 (land) | Germany |\n\n### Pacific / Oceania\n\n| Year | Event | Nature | Territory | Area (approx. km²) | Resulting Polity |\n|------|-------|--------|-----------|---------------------|------------------|\n| 1874 | British annexation | Colony | Fiji | ~18,300 | Britain |\n| 1884 | Protectorate | Colony | British New Guinea (southeastern Papua) | ~147,000 | Britain (transferred to Australia 1906) |\n| 1884 | Protectorate | Colony | German New Guinea (northeastern) | ~240,000 | Germany |\n| 1888 | Protectorate | Colony | Cook Islands | ~240 | Britain (NZ from 1901) |\n| 1893 | Protectorate | Colony | Solomon Islands (British) | ~28,900 | Britain |\n| 1898 | Annexation | Annexation | Hawaii | ~28,300 | United States |\n| 1899 | Tripartite Convention | Partition | Samoa divided: Western Samoa (Germany), Eastern Samoa (USA) | Western ~2,800; Eastern ~200 | Germany; USA |\n| 1900–01 | Boxer Protocol aftermath | — | — | — | — |\n| 1906 | Papua transferred | Transfer | British New Guinea → Territory of Papua | ~147,000 | Australia |\n| 1914 | WWI conquest | Conquest | German New Guinea, Nauru, Western Samoa | Various | Australia, New Zealand, Japan (mandates 1920) |\n| 1920 | League Mandate (Class C) | Mandate | Western Samoa | ~2,800 | New Zealand |\n| 1920 | League Mandate (Class C) | Mandate | Nauru | ~21 | Britain/Australia/NZ |\n| 1920 | League Mandate (Class C) | Mandate | German New Guinea + Bismarck Archipelago | ~240,000 | Australia |\n\n---\n\n## 6. THE AMERICAS (1860–1920)\n\n| Year | Event | Nature | Territory | Area (approx. km²) | Resulting Polity |\n|------|-------|--------|-----------|---------------------|------------------|\n| 1861–65 | US Civil War | Secession then reunification | Confederate States of America (11 states) | ~2,000,000 | CSA exists 1861–1865; reunified as USA |\n| 1865 | Dominican Restoration War | Re-independence | Dominican Republic (Spain had re-annexed 1861) | ~48,700 | Dominican Republic (independent again) |\n| 1867 (Mar 30) | Alaska Purchase (Treaty of Cession) | Purchase/Cession (Russia to USA) | Alaska | ~1,518,000 | United States |\n| 1867 | Canadian Confederation (Jul 1) | Federation | Ontario, Quebec, Nova Scotia, New Brunswick | ~3,500,000 (eventual) | Dominion of Canada |\n| 1870 | Manitoba created | Province entry | Manitoba + Northwest Territories transferred from Hudson's Bay Company | ~1,000,000+ (HBC Rupert's Land) | Canada |\n| 1871 | British Columbia joins Canada | Federation | British Columbia | ~945,000 | Canada |\n| 1879–1884 | War of the Pacific | Conquest/Cession | Chile vs. Bolivia & Peru | — | — |\n| 1883 | Treaty of Ancón (Oct 20) | Cession (Peru to Chile) | Tarapacá Province | ~59,000 | Chile (permanent) |\n| 1884 | Treaty of Valparaíso (Apr 4) | Truce/De facto cession (Bolivia to Chile) | Atacama (Antofagasta) — Bolivia's littoral department | ~120,000 | Chile occupies (Bolivia becomes landlocked; formalized in 1904 Treaty of Peace) |\n| 1883/84 | War of the Pacific | Chilean administration | Tacna and Arica (from Peru) | ~24,000 | Chile administers (Tacna returned to Peru 1929; Arica stays Chilean) |\n| 1889 | Brazilian Republic | Internal transformation | Brazil | ~8,500,000 | Republic of Brazil |\n| 1895 | Venezuela Crisis / Arbitration | Boundary | British Guiana-Venezuela border | — | Mostly awarded to Britain (1899 arbitral award) |\n| 1898 | Spanish-American War / Treaty of Paris | Cession (Spain to USA) | Cuba (independence under US supervision); Puerto Rico; Philippines; Guam | Cuba ~110,000; PR ~9,100; Philippines ~300,000; Guam ~540 | Cuba independent (Platt Amendment); PR, Philippines, Guam → USA |\n| 1899 | US-Germany-UK partition | Partition | Samoan Islands | (see Pacific) | USA gets Eastern Samoa |\n| 1903 (Nov 3) | Panamanian independence | Secession (from Colombia, US-backed) | Panama | ~75,400 | Republic of Panama |\n| 1903 (Nov 18) | Hay-Bunau-Varilla Treaty | Lease/Zone | Panama Canal Zone | ~1,430 | United States (sovereign rights in zone) |\n| 1903 | Treaty of Petrópolis (Nov 17) | Cession/Purchase (Bolivia to Brazil) | Acre Territory | ~191,000 | Brazil (Acre) |\n| 1904 | Treaty of Peace (Oct 20) | Cession (Bolivia to Chile, formalizing 1884) | Atacama/Litoral | ~120,000 | Chile (final) |\n| 1916 | Treaty of the Danish West Indies (Aug 4) / ratified 1917 | Purchase (Denmark to USA) | US Virgin Islands (St. Thomas, St. John, St. Croix) | ~350 | United States (transferred Jan 1917) |\n| 1917 | Jones Act | Status change | Puerto Rico | ~9,100 | US citizens; unincorporated territory |\n\n---\n\n## 7. OTTOMAN EMPIRE — DECLINE AND LOSSES (1860–1920)\n\n*(Many entries already covered in Balkans and Africa sections; this consolidates Ottoman-specific losses.)*\n\n| Year | Event | Nature | Territory Lost | Area (approx. km²) | New Controller |\n|------|-------|--------|---------------|---------------------|----------------|\n| 1861 | Mount Lebanon Mutasarrifate | Autonomy | Mount Lebanon | ~4,500 | Autonomous (international guarantee) |\n| 1878 | Treaty of Berlin | Various | Romania, Serbia, Montenegro (independent); Bulgaria (autonomous); Bosnia (Austrian-occupied); Cyprus (British-administered); Kars/Ardahan/Batumi (Russia) | See Balkans section | Various |\n| 1881 | Convention of Constantinople | Cession | Thessaly + Arta to Greece | ~13,400 | Greece |\n| 1882 | British occupation | De facto loss | Egypt | ~1,000,000 | Britain (de facto) |\n| 1898 | Crete | De facto loss | Crete (autonomous under international protection) | ~8,300 | International; de facto Greek |\n| 1908 | Bosnian Crisis | Formal loss | Bosnia-Herzegovina formally annexed by Austria-Hungary | ~51,000 | Austria-Hungary |\n| 1908 | Bulgarian independence | Formal loss | Bulgaria no longer tributary | ~97,000 | Kingdom of Bulgaria |\n| 1911–12 | Italo-Turkish War | Cession | Libya (Tripolitania, Cyrenaica, Fezzan) + Dodecanese Islands | Libya ~1,760,000; Dodecanese ~2,700 | Italy |\n| 1912–13 | Balkan Wars | Cession | Nearly all European territories except Constantinople + eastern Thrace | ~170,000 lost | Serbia, Greece, Bulgaria, Montenegro, Albania |\n| 1914 | WWI begins | Annexation by Britain | Egypt (formal protectorate declared); Cyprus (annexed) | Egypt ~1,000,000; Cyprus ~9,250 | Britain |\n| 1916 | Arab Revolt begins | Loss of control | Hejaz | ~400,000 | Kingdom of Hejaz (1916–1925) |\n| 1917–18 | British conquest | Conquest | Mesopotamia (Iraq), Palestine, Syria | Iraq ~435,000; Palestine ~27,000; Syria ~185,000 | British/French military occupation |\n| 1918 | Armistice of Mudros (Oct 30) | Armistice/Occupation | Ottoman Empire effectively collapses | — | Allied occupation |\n| 1920 | Treaty of Sèvres (Aug 10) | Partition (never fully implemented) | Armenia (independent); Kurdistan (autonomous); Thrace to Greece; Smyrna/Izmir zone to Greece; Italian and French zones in Anatolia; Arab territories as mandates | Massive losses | Various (mostly reversed by Lausanne 1923) |\n\n---\n\n## 8. WORLD WAR I AND AFTERMATH (1914–1920)\n\n### Treaty of Versailles (Jun 28, 1919) — Germany\n\n| Territory | Nature | Area (approx. km²) | New Holder |\n|-----------|--------|---------------------|------------|\n| Alsace-Lorraine | Return/Cession to France | ~14,500 | France |\n| Eupen-Malmedy | Cession to Belgium | ~1,036 | Belgium |\n| North Schleswig | Plebiscite (1920) → cession to Denmark | ~3,984 | Denmark |\n| South Schleswig | Plebiscite → remains German | ~3,700 | Germany |\n| Posen (Poznań) / West Prussia | Cession to Poland (\"Polish Corridor\") | ~46,000 | Poland |\n| Upper Silesia | Plebiscite (1921) → partitioned | ~13,000 (to Poland); ~17,000 (to Germany) | Poland/Germany |\n| Danzig (Gdańsk) | Free City | ~1,966 | Free City of Danzig (League of Nations) |\n| Memelland (Klaipėda) | Detached, later Lithuanian | ~2,400 | Allied administration (Lithuania seizes 1923) |\n| Hultschin (Hlučín) area | Cession to Czechoslovakia | ~316 | Czechoslovakia |\n| Saar Basin | League administration (15 years) | ~1,900 | League of Nations (plebiscite 1935 → Germany) |\n| German colonies | Mandates | See Africa/Pacific sections | Various |\n\n**Total German territorial losses in Europe: ~65,000 km², ~7 million people.**\n\n### Treaty of Saint-Germain-en-Laye (Sep 10, 1919) — Austria (dissolution of Austria-Hungary)\n\n| Territory | Nature | Area (approx. km²) | New Holder |\n|-----------|--------|---------------------|------------|\n| Bohemia, Moravia, Austrian Silesia | Cession | Bohemia ~52,000; Moravia ~22,000; Silesia ~5,000 | Czechoslovakia |\n| Galicia (western + eastern) | Cession | ~78,000 | Poland (western Galicia) and initially disputed eastern Galicia |\n| Bukovina | Cession | ~10,400 | Romania |\n| South Tyrol + Trentino + Trieste + Istria + parts of Carinthia/Carniola | Cession | South Tyrol ~7,400; Trentino ~6,200; Trieste+Istria ~8,600; parts of Carniola ~9,000 | Italy |\n| Slovenia (most of Carniola, southern Styria, southern Carinthia after plebiscite) | Cession | ~16,000 | Kingdom of Serbs, Croats and Slovenes (Yugoslavia) |\n| Dalmatia | Cession | ~12,700 | Kingdom of SCS (Yugoslavia) / partly Italy |\n| Bosnia-Herzegovina | Cession | ~51,000 | Kingdom of SCS (Yugoslavia) |\n| Remnant | Republic | ~84,000 | Republic of Austria (Deutsch-Österreich) |\n\n### Treaty of Trianon (Jun 4, 1920) — Hungary\n\n| Territory | Nature | Area (approx. km²) | Population Lost | New Holder |\n|-----------|--------|---------------------|-----------------|------------|\n| Transylvania + eastern Banat + parts of Crișana and Maramureș | Cession | ~103,000 | ~5.3 million | Romania |\n| Slovakia + Subcarpathian Ruthenia | Cession | Slovakia ~49,000; Ruthenia ~13,000 | ~3.5 million | Czechoslovakia |\n| Croatia-Slavonia + Vojvodina (western Banat, Bačka, Baranja) + Prekmurje | Cession | ~63,000 | ~4.1 million | Kingdom of SCS (Yugoslavia) |\n| Burgenland | Cession | ~4,000 | ~290,000 | Austria |\n| Fiume (Rijeka) | Disputed | ~21 (city) | ~50,000 | Free State of Fiume (1920–24), then Italy |\n| Remnant Hungary | Republic/Regency | ~93,000 | ~7.6 million | Kingdom of Hungary (Regency) |\n\n**Hungary lost ~72% of its pre-war territory (~325,000 → ~93,000 km²) and ~64% of its population.**\n\n### Treaty of Neuilly-sur-Seine (Nov 27, 1919) — Bulgaria\n\n| Territory | Nature | Area (approx. km²) | New Holder |\n|-----------|--------|---------------------|------------|\n| Western Thrace (Aegean access) | Cession | ~8,600 | Greece (via Allied administration; confirmed 1920) |\n| Western borderlands (Strumica, Tsaribrod, Bosilegrad) | Cession | ~2,500 | Kingdom of SCS (Yugoslavia) |\n| Southern Dobruja | Already lost (1913) | ~7,700 | Romania (confirmed) |\n\n### New States Created / Reconstituted (1917–1920)\n\n| Year | Polity | Nature | Territory | Area (approx. km²) |\n|------|--------|--------|-----------|---------------------|\n| 1917 (Dec 6) | **Finland** | Independence (from Russia) | Finland | ~338,000 |\n| 1918 (Feb 24) | **Estonia** | Independence (from Russia) | Estonia | ~45,000 |\n| 1918 (Feb 16) | **Lithuania** | Independence (from Russia/Germany) | Lithuania | ~65,000 (eventually with Vilnius question) |\n| 1918 (Nov 18) | **Latvia** | Independence (from Russia/Germany) | Latvia | ~65,000 |\n| 1918 (Nov 11) | **Poland** | Reconstitution | Former Russian, German, Austrian partitioned territories | ~389,000 (by 1921, after wars) |\n| 1918 (Oct 28) | **Czechoslovakia** | Independence (from Austria-Hungary) | Bohemia, Moravia, Silesia, Slovakia, Subcarpathian Ruthenia | ~141,000 |\n| 1918 (Dec 1) | **Kingdom of Serbs, Croats and Slovenes (Yugoslavia)** | Unification | Serbia + Montenegro + former Austro-Hungarian South Slavic territories | ~248,000 |\n| 1918 (Dec 1) | **Romania (Greater Romania)** | Unification/Expansion | Old Kingdom + Transylvania + Bessarabia + Bukovina + Banat share | ~295,000 (from ~138,000) |\n| 1918 (Nov 12) | **Republic of Austria** | Rump state | German-speaking Alpine lands | ~84,000 |\n| 1918 (Nov 16) | **Hungary** | Rump state (after several regime changes) | Magyar core | ~93,000 (by 1920) |\n| 1918 | **Ukraine** (briefly) | Independence declared | Ukrainian People's Republic | ~600,000 (contested; mostly absorbed by Soviet Russia/Poland by 1921) |\n| 1918 (May 26) | **Georgia** | Independence (from Russia) | Georgia | ~70,000 (until Soviet conquest 1921) |\n| 1918 (May 28) | **Armenia** | Independence (from Russia) | First Republic of Armenia | ~70,000 (initially; reduced; Soviet 1920) |\n| 1918 (May 28) | **Azerbaijan** | Independence (from Russia) | Azerbaijan Democratic Republic | ~87,000 (until Soviet conquest 1920) |\n\n---\n\n## 9. RUSSIAN EMPIRE / SOVIET CHANGES (1858–1920)\n\n| Year | Event | Nature | Territory | Area (approx. km²) | Resulting Polity |\n|------|-------|--------|-----------|---------------------|------------------|\n| 1858–60 | Treaties of Aigun & Peking | Cession (China to Russia) | Amur region + Primorsky Krai (Outer Manchuria) | ~1,000,000 | Russian Empire |\n| 1864–85 | Central Asian conquests | Conquest | Khanate of Kokand (1876), Emirate of Bukhara (protectorate 1868), Khanate of Khiva (protectorate 1873), Turkmen territories (Geok Tepe 1881, Merv 1884) | ~4,000,000+ total (Central Asia) | Russian Turkestan |\n| 1867 | Alaska sale | Cession (Russia to USA) | Alaska | ~1,518,000 | United States |\n| 1878 | Treaty of Berlin | Acquisition | Kars, Ardahan, Batumi | ~25,000 | Russian Empire |\n| 1878 | Treaty of Berlin | Recovery | Southern Bessarabia | ~6,000 | Russian Empire |\n| 1905 | Treaty of Portsmouth | Cession (Russia to Japan) | Southern Sakhalin | ~36,000 | Japan |\n| 1905 | Treaty of Portsmouth | Lease transfer | Kwantung Leased Territory | ~3,500 | Japan |\n| 1917 | Revolution / independence declarations | Losses begin | Finland, Ukraine, Baltic states, Caucasus, Poland, Bessarabia | See below | Various |\n| 1917 (Dec 6) | Finnish independence | Secession | Finland | ~338,000 | Republic of Finland |\n| 1918 (Feb) | Treaty of Brest-Litovsk (Mar 3) | Cession (forced, later annulled) | Poland, Lithuania, Latvia, Estonia, Finland, Ukraine, parts of Belarus and Caucasus | ~1,300,000+ (ceded territories) | Germany/Austria-Hungary/Ottoman occupation; independent states |\n| 1918 | Post-Brest-Litovsk | Independence | Estonia (~45,000), Latvia (~65,000), Lithuania (~65,000) | ~175,000 total | Independent republics |\n| 1918 (Mar–Apr) | Bessarabia union | Unification (with Romania) | Bessarabia (Sfatul Țării votes union with Romania) | ~44,000 | Romania |\n| 1918 (May) | Caucasus independence | Secession | Georgia, Armenia, Azerbaijan | ~227,000 total | Three independent republics |\n| 1920 (Feb 2) | Treaty of Tartu (Estonia) | Peace/Recognition | Estonia confirmed | ~45,000 | Estonia |\n| 1920 (Aug 11) | Treaty of Riga (Latvia) | Peace/Recognition | Latvia confirmed | ~65,000 | Latvia |\n| 1920 (Jul 12) | Treaty of Moscow (Lithuania) | Peace/Recognition | Lithuania (including Vilnius, which Poland later seized) | ~55,000 | Lithuania |\n| 1920 | Treaty of Tartu (Finland, Oct 14) | Peace/Recognition | Finland + Petsamo corridor | ~338,000 + Petsamo ~10,500 | Finland |\n| 1920–21 | Soviet reconquest | Reconquest | Georgia (Feb 1921), Armenia (Nov 1920), Azerbaijan (Apr 1920) | ~227,000 | Soviet Russia (later USSR) |\n| 1920 | Polish-Soviet War | War | Eastern borderlands of former Russian Empire | — | Treaty of Riga (1921): Poland gains western Belarus/Ukraine (~135,000 km²) |\n\n---\n\n## 10. OTTOMAN/MIDDLE EAST MANDATES (1918–1920)\n\n### League of Nations Mandates (Class A) — assigned 1920, formally 1922–23\n\n| Year Assigned | Mandate | Mandatory Power | Area (approx. km²) | Notes |\n|---------------|---------|-----------------|---------------------|-------|\n| 1920 (San Remo Conference, Apr) | Palestine | Britain | ~27,000 | Includes future Transjordan initially |\n| 1920 | Transjordan | Britain (separated from Palestine 1921) | ~89,000 | Emirate under Abdullah |\n| 1920 | Mesopotamia (Iraq) | Britain | ~435,000 | Kingdom of Iraq under Faisal (1921) |\n| 1920 | Syria | France | ~185,000 | French mandate |\n| 1920 | Lebanon | France (separated from Syria) | ~10,400 | Greater Lebanon (État du Grand Liban, 1920) |\n| 1916 | Kingdom of Hejaz | Independent (under Sharif Hussein) | ~400,000 | Independent until Saudi conquest 1925 |\n\n### Additional Ottoman/Middle East\n\n| Year | Event | Nature | Territory | Area (approx. km²) | Resulting Polity |\n|------|-------|--------|-----------|---------------------|------------------|\n| 1869 | Suez Canal opens | — | — | — | Egypt (British influence grows) |\n| 1878 | Cyprus Convention | Administration | Cyprus | ~9,250 | British administration |\n| 1882 | British occupation of Egypt | Occupation | Egypt | ~1,000,000 | British de facto control |\n| 1899 | Kuwait Agreement | Protectorate | Kuwait | ~18,000 | British protectorate |\n| 1914 | British protectorate over Egypt | Formal protectorate | Egypt | ~1,000,000 | British protectorate (Ottoman suzerainty ended) |\n| 1914 | British annexation | Annexation | Cyprus | ~9,250 | British Crown Colony |\n| 1916 | Sykes-Picot Agreement (May 16) | Secret partition plan | Ottoman Arab territories | — | British/French zones of influence defined |\n| 1916 | Arab Revolt (Jun 10) | Independence | Hejaz | ~400,000 | Kingdom of Hejaz |\n| 1917 | Balfour Declaration (Nov 2) | Policy statement | Palestine | ~27,000 | Commits Britain to Jewish homeland |\n| 1920 | Treaty of Sèvres (Aug 10) | Partition of Ottoman Empire | Armenia (Wilsonian), Kurdistan (autonomous), Smyrna (Greek), Thrace (Greek), Italian/French zones in Anatolia | Massive | Largely unimplemented; superseded by Treaty of Lausanne (1923) |\n\n---\n\n## 11. MISCELLANEOUS TERRITORIAL CHANGES\n\n| Year | Event | Nature | Territory | Area (approx. km²) | Resulting Polity |\n|------|-------|--------|-----------|---------------------|------------------|\n| 1860 | Convention of Peking | Cession (China to Russia) | Kowloon (to Britain); Vladivostok area (to Russia) | See earlier entries | — |\n| 1861 | Unification of Wallachia & Moldavia | Unification | United Principalities → Romania | ~130,000 | Romania (Principality, Kingdom 1881) |\n| 1864 | Ionian Islands | Cession (Britain to Greece) | Ionian Islands (Corfu, etc.) | ~2,300 | Greece |\n| 1867 | Austro-Hungarian Compromise (Ausgleich) | Internal restructuring | Austrian Empire → Austria-Hungary (Dual Monarchy) | ~680,000 | Austria-Hungary |\n| 1871 | Abolition of feudal domains (Japan) | Internal reform | Han system abolished | — | Prefectures of Japan |\n| 1884 | British protectorate | Colony | British Somaliland | ~176,000 | Britain |\n| 1886 | Annexation of Burma | Conquest | Upper Burma | ~677,000 (all Burma) | British India |\n| 1898 | Spanish-American War | Cession | Cuba, Puerto Rico, Philippines, Guam | See Americas | USA / Cuban Republic |\n| 1899 | Condominium | Joint administration | Anglo-Egyptian Sudan | ~2,506,000 | Britain/Egypt |\n| 1905 | Norway independence | Dissolution of union | Norway (from Sweden-Norway) | ~324,000 | Kingdom of Norway |\n| 1907 | New Zealand Dominion | Status change | New Zealand | ~268,000 | Dominion of New Zealand |\n| 1910 | Union of South Africa | Unification | 4 British colonies | ~1,221,000 | Union of South Africa (Dominion) |\n| 1910 | Annexation of Korea | Annexation | Korea | ~221,000 | Japan |\n| 1911–12 | Italo-Turkish War | Conquest/Cession | Libya + Dodecanese | ~1,762,700 | Italy |\n| 1912 | French protectorate | Protectorate | Morocco | ~447,000 | France/Spain |\n| 1916 | Easter Rising | Failed secession | Ireland | — | Remains British (until 1922) |\n| 1918 | Iceland sovereignty | Personal union | Iceland (sovereign under Danish crown) | ~103,000 | Kingdom of Iceland (personal union with Denmark) |\n\n---\n\n## SUMMARY STATISTICS\n\n**Major polities that ceased to exist (1860–1920):**\n- Kingdom of the Two Sicilies (1860)\n- Duchy of Modena (1860)\n- Duchy of Parma (1860)\n- Grand Duchy of Tuscany (1860)\n- Papal States (temporal power, 1870)\n- Kingdom of Hanover (1866)\n- Electorate of Hesse (1866)\n- Duchy of Nassau (1866)\n- Free City of Frankfurt (1866)\n- Ryukyu Kingdom (1879)\n- Zulu Kingdom (1879)\n- Orange Free State (1902, as independent; revived briefly)\n- South African Republic / Transvaal (1902)\n- Congo Free State (1908, to Belgium)\n- Korean Empire (1910)\n- Kingdom of Montenegro (1918, into Yugoslavia)\n- Austria-Hungary (1918, dissolved)\n- German Empire (1918, becomes Weimar Republic)\n- Russian Empire (1917, becomes Soviet Russia)\n- Ottoman Empire (1918–22, becomes Republic of Turkey 1923)\n\n**Major polities created (1860–1920):**\n- Kingdom of Italy (1861)\n- Dominion of Canada (1867)\n- North German Confederation (1867)\n- German Empire (1871)\n- Bulgaria (1878 autonomous, 1908 independent)\n- Serbia (1878 independent, 1882 kingdom)\n- Romania (1878 independent, 1881 kingdom)\n- Montenegro (1878 independent, 1910 kingdom)\n- Republic of Brazil (1889)\n- Republic of Cuba (1902)\n- Republic of Panama (1903)\n- Kingdom of Norway (1905)\n- Union of South Africa (1910)\n- Republic of China (1912)\n- Albania (1913)\n- Finland (1917)\n- Estonia (1918)\n- Latvia (1918)\n- Lithuania (1918)\n- Poland (1918)\n- Czechoslovakia (1918)\n- Kingdom of SCS / Yugoslavia (1918)\n- Austria (1918)\n- Hungary (1918/1920)\n- Ukraine (1918, briefly)\n- Georgia (1918)\n- Armenia (1918)\n- Azerbaijan (1918)\n- Kingdom of Hejaz (1916)\n- Weimar Republic / Germany (1919)\n- Free City of Danzig (1920)\n\n---\n\n## KEY CAVEATS FOR DATABASE VERIFICATION\n\n1. **Dates of formal treaties vs. effective control often differ.** For example, Bosnia-Herzegovina was occupied by Austria-Hungary from 1878 but formally annexed in 1908. Your database should track both dates.\n\n2. **Area figures are approximations** drawn from commonly cited historical sources. Colonial boundaries in Africa were often vaguely defined and shifted over decades; the figures given represent eventual extents.\n\n3. **The Treaty of Sevres (1920) was never ratified by Turkey** and was superseded by the Treaty of Lausanne (1923). If your database ends at 1920, Sevres represents the intended partition; Lausanne represents the actual outcome.\n\n4. **Poland's borders were not finalized until 1921** (Treaty of Riga with Soviet Russia, Upper Silesia plebiscite). The 1918–1920 period represents ongoing territorial formation.\n\n5. **The League of Nations mandates were assigned at San Remo (April 1920) but formally confirmed in 1922–1923.** Your database should note both the assignment date and the formal confirmation.\n\n6. **Several African colonial boundaries were adjusted repeatedly** through bilateral treaties (e.g., Anglo-French, Anglo-German, Franco-German conventions throughout the 1890s–1900s). The entries above capture the major ones but not every minor boundary rectification.\n\n7. **Bessarabia's union with Romania (1918)** was not recognized by Soviet Russia and remained contested. Similarly, the Baltic states' independence was contested until the Soviet-Baltic peace treaties of 1920.\n\nThis catalog covers approximately **150+ distinct territorial changes** across the 1860–1920 period and should serve as a comprehensive reference for verifying polity records in your academic database."}],"stop_reason":"end_turn","stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":3,"cache_creation_input_tokens":8627,"cache_read_input_tokens":0,"output_tokens":15146,"server_tool_use":{"web_search_requests":0,"web_fetch_requests":0},"service_tier":"standard","cache_creation":{"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":8627},"inference_geo":"","iterations":[],"speed":"standard"}},"requestId":"req_011CZNX39Lj4y7oYuu2z9vsg","type":"assistant","uuid":"6729845e-96ba-47e0-86d9-c130d635d09a","timestamp":"2026-03-24T20:19:37.185Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/catalin/WHEP-refactor-polities","sessionId":"b5ea2b37-3948-44f5-9e01-2ef45e84c64c","version":"2.1.81","gitBranch":"lbm364dl/refactor-polities","slug":"sequential-inventing-treehouse"}
